is_feature_attack: If true, applies perturbations to node features; otherwise, perturbs the graph structure (edges)
element_idx: Index of the element to attack
epsilon: Magnitude of the perturbation — higher values result in stronger attacks

budget: Attack budget — maximum number of allowed perturbations (edges or features)
perturb_features: Indicates whether the features can be changed
perturb_structure: Indicates whether the structure can be changed
direct: True for direct attacks on the target node; False allows only indirect attacks via neighboring nodes
depth: Depth of the indirect attack — how far from the target node changes are allowed
delta_cutoff: Threshold for log-likelihood ratio test to ensure structural perturbations remain stealthy
surrogate_train_ratio: Fraction of nodes used to train the surrogate model
surrogate_epochs: Number of training epochs for the surrogate model

n_perturbations: Number of perturbations. If None, then n_perturbations = degree(node_idx)
perturb_features: Indicates whether the features can be changed
perturb_structure: Indicates whether the structure can be changed
direct: Indicates whether to directly modify edges/features of the node attacked or only those of influencers
n_influencers: Number of influencing nodes. Will be ignored if direct is True

individual_size: Number of rewiring operations within one gene
generations: Number of generations for genetic algorithm
prob_cross: Probability of crossover between two genes

is_feature_attack: If true, applies perturbations to node features; otherwise, perturbs the graph structure (edges)
epsilon: Maximum allowed perturbation — defines the attack strength or budget
element_idx: Index of the element to attack
learning_rate: Learning rate used for optimizing perturbations during the attack
num_iterations: Number of optimization steps for generating adversarial perturbations

node_idx: Index of the node being attacked

element_idx: Index of the element to attack
eps: Parameter responsible for the proportion of edges that can be rewired
epochs: Number of epochs of edge rewiring policy training
mlp_hidden: Dimensionality of the hidden layer of the perceptron used in policy learning
h_method: h() function type for edge representation from article
pooling_method: A method for handling vertex embeddings for graph representation
